account.description: Startup building developer tools, APIs, infrastructure, observability, data platforms, security, code tools, agents, AI apps, AI infrastructure, evals, or named demo-category devtools/AI products.
Startup has evidence of employees, funding, customers, or an operating team beyond a purely speculative side project. More than 10 employees is a strong signal, not a hard requirement.
Company has an owned software product, dashboard, API, workflow, or developer-facing product experience.
Company enrichment is present enough to judge category, product surface, employee count, and startup context.
Person attended the Resend DevTools Meetup. This is warm context only and does not prove fit by itself.

person.description: Person is a founder, co-founder, CEO, CTO, technical founder, or similarly accountable startup operator.
Person works in engineering, product engineering, platform, infrastructure, frontend, full-stack, AI engineering, developer experience, or related technical implementation.
Person works in DevRel, PMM, growth, GTM, partnerships, community, or related market-building work.
Person owns an agency or studio with clear DevRel, PMM, growth, GTM, engineering, or implementation relevance to devtools/AI startups.
There is current LinkedIn-backed evidence for the relevant role and company.
Trusted CRM, customer, opportunity, contact, or relationship evidence shows the operator already has a strong existing relationship with this person.

account.description: Resend or another host, speaker, organizer, or explicitly excluded competitor account.
Large non-startup, school, nonprofit, investor, professional services firm, or unrelated organization with no startup/product signal.
Business appears unrelated to software, AI, devtools, data, infrastructure, or product workflows.
Company category, product surface, startup status, or employee count cannot be judged from available enrichment.

person.description: Person is already in the operator's CRM, customer, opportunity, contact, or relationship source of truth.
Investor, advisor, or ecosystem role without direct company operating, buying, implementation, or GTM ownership.
Student, intern, incoming intern, or too early-career to be useful for this demo lead list.
Person is a meetup speaker, host, organizer, or host-company employee rather than an attendee lead.
Generic sales, account executive, SDR, BDR, or sales-only role without DevRel, PMM, growth, GTM, product, technical, devtools, AI, or startup-operator evidence.
Freelancer or solo consultant without agency-owner, startup-operator, or devtools/AI GTM relevance.
Primary role is finance, investment, HR, recruiting, legal, real estate, or another function with no relevant operating, technical, or GTM path.
Only a name, weak inferred identity, or non-current role evidence is available.

tier_1.label: founders_of_devtools_ai_startups
tier_1.definition: Founders of devtools or AI startups, including named demo-category companies.

tier_2.label: operators_at_devtools_ai_startups
tier_2.definition: DevRel, PMM, growth/GTM operators, engineers, or relevant agency owners at devtools or AI startups.

tier_3.label: other_startup_founders
tier_3.definition: Startup founders whose companies are not clearly devtools or AI, with team size or operating traction used as confidence evidence rather than a hard gate.

fit_logic.high: Clear current evidence supports the required account or person rules with no hard exclusions.
fit_logic.medium: Relevant evidence exists, but category, employee count, role, or freshness is incomplete.
fit_logic.low: Hard exclusion matched, evidence is too sparse, or the person/account does not match this demo ICP.

confidence_logic.high: LinkedIn plus current company evidence are present, and company enrichment supports the account category.
confidence_logic.medium: LinkedIn or current company evidence is present, but company enrichment or category confidence is incomplete.
confidence_logic.low: Sparse, inferred, stale, ambiguous, or name-only evidence.

tier_logic.global: Hard exclusions force tier 0. Do not assign tier 1, 2, or 3 unless account_fit and person_fit are medium or high.
tier_logic.tier_1: Use for founders of devtools/AI startups.
tier_logic.tier_2: Use for DevRel, PMM, growth/GTM operators, engineers, or relevant agency owners at devtools/AI startups.
tier_logic.tier_3: Use for other startup founders whose companies are not clearly devtools or AI. More than 10 employees raises confidence, but do not force otherwise qualified startup founders to tier 0 solely because employee count is missing or smaller.
tier_logic.tier_0: Use for hard exclusions, known-contact people, name-only records, wrong functions, non-startups, non-software businesses, investors, students, speakers, hosts, generic sales, freelancers, or insufficient evidence.
